post.content:
Current Status: 5 years experience
Position: SSE2 at MakeMyTrip
Location: Bangalore

Reached out to HR on LinkedIn.

Round 1: Interviewer pasted the problem on the coding platform, and explained it. I asked many questions to clarify. The question is given below. Later, they extended it by providing the string in large files, and then asked to modify the solution. I needed to write only the solution for the first problem and explain the approach for large inputs.

Verify whether a long text is following the order rule defined in order string. For example the order string is "abcd", which means "a" can't appear at any position after "b", "c" and "d" in the text, "b" can't appear at any position after "c" and "d" in the text and "c" can't appear at any position after "d" in the text. if the text is "axubbxcxbxd", then the text didn't follow the rule, since "b" appears after "c" in substring "cxb". 

Round 2: Design Meeting scheduler. Asked a lot of questions around it. Discussed the approach and wrote the solution. This was more of a machine coding question. 

Round 3: Hiring Manager Round. Discussed the design of a project I worked on in my company. Further, discussed on what further optimizations and improvements that can be made in the architecture if the scale increases by 100x. Further, the manager asked a few behavioral questions.

Round 4: High Level System Design. Was asked to design a restaurant listing system on the basis of user's current location. Discussed a lot on Geohashing, database choice and database architecture. 

Got the response from the HR the next day that I have cleared all the rounds and got the offer 2 days later.

Compensation details:
https://leetcode.com/discuss/compensation/2852932/Phonepe-vs-Uber-L4

Congratulations ! Have a couple of questions :

1. How many days after first two interviews, does the next two happen
2. What were the key areas of focus in the System Design round : was it more on DB choice, or did they go into reliability and fault tolerance as well ?

\'Verify whether a long text is following the order rule defined in order string. For example the order string is "abcd", which means "a" can\'t appear at any position after "b", "c" and "d" in the text, "b" can\'t appear at any position after "c" and "d" in the text and "c" can\'t appear at any position after "d" in the text. if the text is "axubbxcxbxd", then the text didn\'t follow the rule, since "b" appears after "c" in substring "cxb".\' \n--> Union Find
Congrats! What is the duration of each round? What was the expectation for the 2nd round?
hi, was there only one DSA round ?
what was your approach for round 1 ?
hi could u please share the question asked in 1st round\n
